summary: Crystal‑clear front‑of‑house audience tape of the final JFK concert captures a blistering set with scorching Scarlet > Fire, soaring Aiko, and a tear‑through Blow Away.
review: The recording is repeatedly praised as crisp, full‑range and almost “you are there,” making this high‑quality FOB audience capture one of the most beloved shows of ’89.  Musically the band is in top form – Hell in a Bucket kicks off a set that rolls through Let It Grow, Blow Away, Scarlet > Fire, The Other One and an inspired Wharf Rat, with Brent’s keyboards stealing the spotlight on several numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews are overwhelmingly positive, many 5‑star, citing both an excellent performance and a high‑fidelity audience/FOB recording; only minor variations (a few 4‑star notes) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house (FOB) audience tape praised for crisp mids, punchy highs and full dynamics; described as “high quality,” “crisp,” “full of dynamics,” and “you are there.” No significant drop‑outs or muffling reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a seamless, high‑energy flow – an explosive first set that builds into a marathon second set with strong transitions (Scarlet > Fire, Wharf Rat) and plenty of crowd‑pleasing jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for scorching guitar work, especially on Scarlet > Fire and the overall “fire” he brings to the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted singing the ‘Box’ segment and providing solid bass foundation; no specific criticism.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned for rhythmic drive (“Bobby does the Throwing Stones riff”) and steady guitar presence throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland singled out for “tearing it up” on Blow Away and delivering powerful keyboard moments across the set.
